new_nodes.gloss: Theon has the miller's two sons from the Acorn Water flayed and tarred and mounts their heads on Winterfell's walls as 'proof' Bran and Rickon are dead — the moral nadir of his ACOK arc (architecture.md's named example of event.deception).
Ramsay imprisons, mutilates, and renames Theon 'Reek' at the Dreadfort, destroying his identity — the captivity hub of the ADWD arc (the Kyra-hunt-escape sub-beat included).

The snowbound killings inside Winterfell (Yellow Dick, Roger Ryswell's groom, a Flint, Aenys's squire) attributed to 'a ghost' — Mance's spearwives' covert work; Little Walder Frey's death the one they disclaim. Distinct from wedding-guests-observed-in-torchlight (the wedding-night node).
Theon reclaims his name, leads Jeyne Poole through the chaos of the Frey-Manderly brawl, and leaps from Winterfell's battlements into the snow — the redemption turn; Holly and Frenya die covering it.

theon-greyjoy.note: Conservative discoverability fix for the flagged cross-identity use case. Add the UNIQUE aliases only — NOT bare 'Reek' (collides with the `reek` node's own name). A disambiguation note in both bodies handles the bare-'Reek' query. requires weirwood-refresh.

node_alias_hygiene.disambiguation_notes: Add a short note to BOTH theon-greyjoy and reek node bodies documenting the three-bearer Reek tangle: Reek (I) = the original servant (the `reek` node); Ramsay-as-Reek (ACOK, modeled `ramsay-snow IMPERSONATES reek`); Reek (III) = Theon (ADWD, this enrichment).

retire_candidates.reason: FACTUALLY FALSE — Bran Stark is alive (a POV character); even in-world the killed boy was a miller's son. The new event.deception node + `bran-stark VICTIM_IN theon-fakes-the-deaths-of-bran-and-rickon` models the truth. fresh-verify gates the drop.
Cross-continent chronology junk (old global-PRECEDES-spine leftover); no causal/narrative link. fresh-verify gates.
Cross-book chronology junk; no causation. fresh-verify gates.
Cross-book chronology junk; no causation. fresh-verify gates.

dropped_at_synthesis: ramsay TORTURES theon-greyjoy — ALREADY EXISTS (lenses wrongly flagged it first-use).
theon PRISONER_OF ramsay-snow / roose-bolton — ALREADY EXIST.
ramsay MANIPULATES theon (renaming) — dropped, redundant with existing TORTURES + the new VICTIM_IN on the breaking node.
frenya VICTIM_IN escape — dropped (death not on-page; frenya DIED_AT winterfell already exists).
object nodes (sea-bitch, smiler, iron-crown, flaying-knife, falchion, wolf-brooch, cloaks) — deferred to harvest/a future descriptive pass; not load-bearing enough for this build dip.
pink-letter artifact node — NOT minted; `bastard-letter` node already exists + `pink-letter-delivered` event already wired.
mance-rayder alias 'Abel' add — NOT needed; already present on mance-rayder.
the reclaim as a standalone `theon-reclaims-his-name` node — folded into the escape node body + quote (too thin for its own node).

edges.quote: By the time they put that arrow through his back, I'd smeared myself with the girl's filth and dressed in his rags.
edges.note: FIRST-USE IMPERSONATES in graph. The cross-identity marquee: Ramsay killed the original servant Reek (in Ramsay's clothes) and took the dead man's name/rags to be captured as 'Reek'. `reek` node stays the servant; this edge ties the ACOK disguise.

edges.quote: The miller's boys had been of an age with Bran and Rickon, alike in size and coloring
edges.note: Theon authors the deception.

edges.quote: once Reek had flayed the skin from their faces and dipped their heads in tar
edges.note: The original `reek` (Ramsay-as-Reek in ACOK) physically flays/tars the faces — consistent with the existing `reek AGENT_IN trail-followed` attribution.

edges.quote: those misshapen lumps of rotting flesh
edges.note: The actual murdered boys (millers-sons node exists).

edges.quote: alike in size and coloring
edges.note: Bran is the person whose death is faked (harm-by-deception). Replaces the false `theon KILLS bran-stark` (see retire R1).

edges.quote: of an age with Bran and Rickon
edges.note: Rickon's death is faked alongside Bran's.

edges.quote: Only Maester Luwin had the stomach to come near.
edges.note: Luwin witnesses the mounted heads; begs to sew them onto the boys' shoulders for the crypts — the moral counterpoint.

edges.quote: Somehow Osha and the wretched boys were eluding him.
edges.note: The failed hunt for the real Stark boys forces Theon to substitute the miller's sons as fake deaths.

edges.quote: If we'd said they were rams' heads, they would have seen horns.
edges.note: The deception propagates as false news to Robb (the more proximate cause than the existing capture->robb edge; flag capture->robb for possible re-point at finalize).

edges.quote: who captured me at Winterfell
edges.note: Theon is captured at the sack and taken to the Dreadfort, where the breaking happens. Cross-arc seam (the ACOK fall feeds the ADWD captivity).

edges.quote: It had been scourged from him, starved from him, flayed from him.
edges.note: Ramsay is the primary agent of the breaking.

edges.quote: he went along as docile as a dog
edges.note: Theon as primary victim of the identity-destruction.

edges.quote: a second bit Kyra on the leg as she scrambled up a hillside
edges.note: The Kyra-hunt (a staged false-escape) is a sub-beat of the Dreadfort captivity; kyra DIED_AT dreadfort already exists.

edges.quote: Sour Alyn. Skinner. Yellow Dick.
edges.note: Skinner is the named flayer among Ramsay's Bastard's Boys who oversee Theon's torture.

edges.quote: he had been down there in the dungeons, but it had to have been half a year at least
edges.note: FIRST-USE IMPRISONED_AT in graph. The location of the captivity.

edges.quote: My lord, Moat Cailin is yours. Here are its last defenders.
edges.note: Reek/Theon talks the ironborn garrison into surrender — his one active deed for the Bolton war effort. Fills the baseline gap (Theon's Moat Cailin role unbuilt).

edges.quote: Three days later, the vanguard of Roose Bolton's host threaded its way through the ruins
edges.note: Clearing the Neck lets Roose's host march north to stage the wedding at Winterfell.

edges.quote: someone had sliced it off and stuffed it into his mouth so forcefully they had broken three of his teeth
edges.note: Yellow Dick is the marquee victim of the snowbound killings.

edges.quote: Do me, the way you did the others. Yellow Dick and the rest. It was you.
edges.note: Abel/Mance commands the spearwives whose covert killings these are. fresh-verify: confirm AGENT_IN vs SUSPECTED_OF given GRRM keeps it coy (Holly deflects). Little Walder's death is explicitly NOT theirs.

edges.quote: Yellow Dick and the rest. It was you.
edges.note: Named spearwife; the group's non-denial. fresh-verify: AGENT_IN vs SUSPECTED_OF.

edges.quote: Hold a dagger? He hardly has the strength to hold a spoon.
edges.note: Theon is suspected in-world (the Freys point at him) and exonerated by Lady Dustin on physical grounds — the suspicion is canon; SUSPECTED_OF never asserts the act.

edges.quote: When the cooks found him outside the kitchens, buried up to his neck in a snowdrift
edges.note: Theon is the POV witness/narrator across the murders chapter.

edges.quote: Ser Hosteen, assemble your knights and men-at-arms by the main gates.
edges.note: The murders fuel the Frey-Manderly brawl; Roose sends both factions out the gates against Stannis, depleting the garrison and opening the escape window.

edges.quote: Theon grabbed Jeyne about the waist and jumped.
edges.note: Theon leads and executes the escape (the leap from the battlements).

edges.quote: Theon grabbed Jeyne about the waist and jumped.
edges.note: Jeyne is the imperiled rescued subject (consistent with her VICTIM_IN on the SUB_BEAT carry node).

edges.quote: Lord Stannis is outside the walls, and not far by the sound of it. All we need do is reach him.
edges.note: Abel/Mance is the architect of the escape (the plan to reach Stannis); sends the spearwives with Theon.

edges.quote: Rowan grasped Theon's arm. "The bath. It must be now."
edges.note: Rowan triggers and coordinates the escape from inside.

edges.quote: her blade slid through the meat of his neck, just below the ear
edges.note: Holly kills the gate guard, opening the passage.

edges.quote: Go on. I will hold the kneelers here.
edges.note: Frenya holds the drawbridge, sacrificing herself to cover the escape.

edges.quote: a second shaft appeared between her breasts. Holly grabbed for the nearest merlon and fell.
edges.note: Holly is shot down by crossbowmen on the inner wall.

edges.quote: Theon grabbed Jeyne about the waist and jumped.
edges.note: Wires the longest-standing dead-end node forward into the escape hub (the carry up the stairs culminates in the leap).

edges.quote: And I want my Reek. Send them to me, bastard
edges.note: THE MARQUEE CROSS-CONTAINER SEAM. The escape triggers Ramsay's pink letter demanding the bride and 'his Reek' back; pink-letter-delivered already ENABLES the-shieldhall-speech, so Theon's arc now wires into Jon's stabbing.

edges.quote: burn the rest. Burn it, burn it all
edges.note: Ramsay's Dreadfort men turn on the ironborn and torch the castle — the attack becomes the sack.

edges.quote: Stannis must march or lose them
edges.note: Roose's strategic reasoning: the false-Arya wedding forces Stannis to march (his clansmen won't abandon 'Ned's daughter'). MOTIVATES routes through the person. Distinct from the existing fight-by-deepwood-motte ENABLES stannis-march.

edges.quote: The noose I wore was not made of hempen rope, that's true enough, but I felt it all the same.
edges.note: The hostage years install the divided-loyalty wound; Theon himself names it as the chafe driving his need to prove himself. MOTIVATES the person.
